items.title: Mamdani says New York lacks authority to arrest Netanyahu, calls him ‘war criminal’
items.description: Israel's prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, is the target of an arrest warrant issued by the International Criminal Court related to the war on Gaza.

items.title: Trump plans steep tariffs on generic drugs starting in 2028 to spur U.S. production
items.description: The phased schedule is intended to push generic drugmakers to move production onshore.

items.title: Japan exports and imports grow at fastest pace in June since November 2022, beating estimates
items.description: Exports climbed 19.3%, while imports spiked 25.4% compared to June last year.

items.title: Sony’s 'ironic' PlayStation disc decision upends gamer conventions and threatens a $7 billion resale market
items.description: In 2013, Sony tweaked Xbox by showing how easy it was to share a PlayStation disc. Now, gamers say Sony has become exactly what it mocked.

items.title: Nike to cut off thousands of online distributors in China, restructure digital footprint
items.description: Nike is streamlining its online business in China as the company looks to stabilize pricing and branding and get back to growth.

items.title: Capital One earnings were good enough but didn't answer the big question hanging over the stock
items.description: Investors want to know when Capital One will begin to see more tangible benefits from its Discover and Brex deals.

items.title: JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon made bearish call on treasury bond market. Many investors already acted on it
items.description: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon warned this week long-term treasuries are not a good buy, even if stocks fall. Many investors already acted on that message this year.

items.title: Prediction markets and their opponents are spending more to influence Congress in 2026
items.description: Kalshi and groups representing the casino and gaming industry and online sports betting apps, all have boosted their lobbying spending in 2026.

items.title: The Fed rang the alarm about Anthropic's Mythos AI model — but had to go months without it
items.description: The central bank didn't have access to Claude Mythos Preview as of mid-July, even as other institutions were racing to patch their vulnerabilities.

items.title: Super Micro surges 15% on new order and margin disclosure after SpaceX announcement
items.description: Many server makers are seeing faster growth as companies race to deploy artificial intelligence servers.

items.title: RFK Jr. says cyclospora outbreak is 'under control'
items.description: Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. on Tuesday said that the ongoing outbreak of cyclosporiasis is "under control."

items.title: BlackRock, Carhartt, Ford and Google launch skilled trades workforce initiative
items.description: The coalition of companies aims to address a growing labor shortage by promoting careers in the skilled trades, the firms said.

items.title: OpenAI appoints two new members to board of directors
items.description: OpenAI appointed longtime financial executives David Vélez and Robin Vince to its nonprofit and for-profit boards.

items.title: White House teases new trade action 'soon' as it works to re-create global tariff regime
items.description: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer telegraphed that the Trump administration could impose new tariffs just as other duties are set to expire.

items.title: Goldman Sachs creates private markets platform as rich investors seek the next SpaceX and Stripe
items.description: Goldman Sachs is launching a new alternative investments platform aimed at giving wealthy clients and family offices direct stakes in private companies.

items.title: $100M New Jersey deli fraudster James Patten sentenced to 21 months in prison
items.description: The notorious New Jersey deli fraud case led to losses of more than $5 million for victims that included two U.S. universities.

items.title: Bets against SpaceX grow to 32% of float as Elon Musk warns short sellers won't survive
items.description: About 206 million SpaceX shares are now sold short, representing roughly 32% of the company's publicly tradable float, according to S3.

items.title: Apple prepares a new way to buy iPhones. Plus, making sense of Google's new AI models
items.description: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

items.title: Time for a crypto comeback? Traders bet that this stock will lead the way
items.description: Crypto bulls have been waiting for a rebound, and traders think this stock could lead the way.

items.title: Investing experts: Why 'just buy the market' doesn't necessarily apply to bonds
items.description: The flagship index for bond investors has some idiosyncracies bond investors should be aware of, experts say.
